title: Shopping Culture & Bargaining Tips for Tourists in China

tips.tip: Bargain at markets and small shops
tips.detail: Bargaining is expected at street markets, souvenir shops, and independent stores. Start at 30-40% of the asking price.

tips.tip: Do not bargain in department stores or malls
tips.detail: Prices in large shopping malls, supermarkets, and chain stores are fixed. Attempting to bargain may be seen as rude.
Look for signs stating '不讲价' (bù jiǎng jià) meaning 'no bargaining.' This is common in modern retail and bookstores.
Inspect electronics, clothes, and ceramics for defects. Test zippers, buttons, and screen functions where possible.
The official tax receipt (fapiao) is essential for warranty claims, returns, or expense reimbursement. Ask for it proactively.
Return policies vary. Large malls usually have a 7-30 day return window with receipt. Small shops may refuse returns.
Extremely cheap electronics, luxury bags, or watches are likely counterfeits. Purchase branded goods only from authorized retailers.

tips.tip: Set up WeChat Pay or Alipay if possible
tips.detail: Mobile payments are ubiquitous. Many small vendors prefer or only accept these apps. Cash is accepted but increasingly rare.

tips.tip: Always carry some cash as a backup
tips.detail: Have smaller bills (10, 20, 50 yuan) for markets, taxis, and places where mobile payment may fail due to network issues.
Many restaurants and services offer steep discounts via group-buying on apps like Meituan. Ask your hotel staff for help.

tips.tip: Bring cash and bargain at night markets
tips.detail: Night markets (yè shì) are for food and trinkets. Prices are negotiable, and cash is still widely used for street food.

tips.tip: Try food before buying a large quantity
tips.detail: You can often sample snacks. Purchase a small portion first to check if you like the taste before buying more.

tips.tip: Use duty-free shops for known brands
tips.detail: Duty-free shops in airports and specific zones offer genuine luxury goods and cosmetics, often at good prices.

tips.tip: Check duty-free allowances for your country
tips.detail: Know the import limits for alcohol, tobacco, and total value to avoid taxes when returning home. Keep all receipts.

tips.tip: Use a calculator or translation app for numbers
tips.detail: Show the price you want to pay on your phone calculator to avoid confusion during price negotiations.
